data.title: Will Eastern Bankshares' New Chief People Officer Redefine Its Talent Edge and Culture Strategy (EBC)?
data.description:
• Eastern Bank recently promoted Jennifer Wambold, formerly Senior Vice President of Total Rewards and Benefits, to Executive Vice President and Chief People Officer, adding her to the bank’s Management Committee after she joined in 2025 to lead compensation, benefits and recognition strategies.
  • Her appointment brings three decades of human resources experience in leading organizations through change to Eastern Bank’s top leadership group, reinforcing the emphasis on aligning culture with data-driven workforce decisions.
  • We’ll now explore how Wambold’s elevation to Chief People Officer could influence Eastern Bankshares’ investment narrative and long-term talent strategy.

Eastern Bankshares Investment Narrative Recap

To own Eastern Bankshares, you need to believe in its ability to translate a regional banking franchise into sustainable earnings while managing commercial real estate credit risk and the after effects of recent one off items. The promotion of Jennifer Wambold to Chief People Officer looks incrementally positive for execution and culture, but it does not materially change the near term focus on asset quality and reserve levels as the key catalyst and risk.

Among recent announcements, the Q4 2025 results, including net charge offs of US$9.8 million or 0.18% of average loans, stand out as most relevant. They provide a current datapoint on credit costs in the commercial real estate book, which remains central to how investors may judge both the sustainability of earnings and the impact of leadership changes on risk management discipline.

data.title: How Stagwell’s (STGW) New Enterprise AI Push Is Reframing Its Martech Investment Story
data.description:
• In late March 2026, Stagwell expanded The Harris Poll’s HarrisQuest brand and reputation tracking platform into Europe and introduced Lou, an AI analyst embedded in HarrisQuest to deliver rapid, self-serve insight generation for marketers and executives across 24 countries.
  • Together with the appointment of Michael Twedell as Stagwell’s first Senior Vice President for Enterprise AI Solutions, these moves highlight a push to embed AI deeper into the company’s SaaS marketing stack and client-facing tools.
  • Next, we’ll examine how the new Enterprise AI leadership role could influence Stagwell’s investment narrative and AI-driven martech ambitions.

The future of work is here. Discover the 34 top robotics and automation stocks leading the charge in AI-driven automation and industrial transformation.

Advertisement

Stagwell Investment Narrative Recap

To own Stagwell, you need to believe its push into proprietary, AI-enabled marketing tools can gradually lift margins and make results less dependent on large cyclical media budgets. The HarrisQuest expansion and Lou launch support this AI and SaaS narrative, but do not materially change the near term balance between the key catalyst of better profitability and the ongoing risks from client concentration, integration complexity, and the uncertain payoff from AI investments.

Among recent developments, the appointment of Michael Twedell as Senior Vice President for Enterprise AI Solutions looks most relevant here, because it centralizes responsibility for commercializing Stagwell’s AI products, including Lou and the wider Marketing Cloud. That role could be an important test of whether the company can turn its growing AI toolkit into stickier, higher margin software and data revenues that help offset risks tied to mega tech client exposure and volatile media spend.

Yet beneath this AI story, investors still need to weigh the risk that heavy reliance on a handful of mega tech clients could...

data.title: Possible Turnaround For Genmin Insiders, Still Down US$926k
data.description:
Insiders who purchased US$3.09m worth of Genmin Limited (ASX:GEN) shares over the past year recouped some of their losses after price gained 17% last week. However, total losses seen by insiders are still US$926k since the time of purchase.

While we would never suggest that investors should base their decisions solely on what the directors of a company have been doing, we would consider it foolish to ignore insider transactions altogether.

We've found 21 US stocks that are forecast to pay a dividend yield of over 6% next year. See the full list for free.

Advertisement

The Last 12 Months Of Insider Transactions At Genmin

In the last twelve months, the biggest single purchase by an insider was when Executive Chair Gregory Lilleyman bought AU$3.1m worth of shares at a price of AU$0.01 per share. That means that an insider was happy to buy shares at above the current price of AU$0.007. Their view may have changed since then, but at least it shows they felt optimistic at the time. We always take careful note of the price insiders pay when purchasing shares. It is encouraging to see an insider paid above the current price for shares, as it suggests they saw value, even at higher levels. The only individual insider to buy over the last year was Gregory Lilleyman.

The chart below shows insider transactions (by companies and individuals) over the last year. If you want to know exactly who sold, for how much, and when, simply click on the graph below!

See our latest analysis for Genmin

There are always plenty of stocks that insiders are buying. If investing in lesser known companies is your style, you could take a look at this free list of companies. (Hint: insiders have been buying them).

Insider Ownership Of Genmin

I like to look at how many shares insiders own in a company, to help inform my view of how aligned they are with insiders. Usually, the higher the insider ownership, the more likely it is that insiders will be incentivised to build the company for the long term. It appears that Genmin insiders own 27% of the company, worth about AU$6.6m. This level of insider ownership is good but just short of being particularly stand-out. It certainly does suggest a reasonable degree of alignment.

So What Does This Data Suggest About Genmin Insiders?

It doesn't really mean much that no insider has traded Genmin shares in the last quarter. But insiders have shown more of an appetite for the stock, over the last year. Insiders own shares in Genmin and we see no evidence to suggest they are worried about the future. So while it's helpful to know what insiders are doing in terms of buying or selling, it's also helpful to know the risks that a particular company is facing. Be aware that Genmin is showing 6 warning signs in our investment analysis, and 5 of those are a bit unpleasant...

Of course, you might find a fantastic investment by looking elsewhere. So take a peek at this free list of interesting companies.

For the purposes of this article, insiders are those individuals who report their transactions to the relevant regulatory body. We currently account for open market transactions and private dispositions of direct interests only, but not derivative transactions or indirect interests.

data.title: We Like f-code's (TSE:9211) Earnings For More Than Just Statutory Profit
data.description:
f-code Inc.'s (TSE:9211) solid earnings announcement recently didn't do much to the stock price. We did some analysis to find out why and believe that investors might be missing some encouraging factors contained in the earnings.

Trump has pledged to "unleash" American oil and gas and these 15 US stocks have developments that are poised to benefit.

Advertisement

The Impact Of Unusual Items On Profit

Importantly, our data indicates that f-code's profit was reduced by JP¥2.4b, due to unusual items, over the last year. While deductions due to unusual items are disappointing in the first instance, there is a silver lining. When we analysed the vast majority of listed companies worldwide, we found that significant unusual items are often not repeated. And, after all, that's exactly what the accounting terminology implies. f-code took a rather significant hit from unusual items in the year to December 2025. All else being equal, this would likely have the effect of making the statutory profit look worse than its underlying earnings power.

That might leave you wondering what analysts are forecasting in terms of future profitability. Luckily, you can click here to see an interactive graph depicting future profitability, based on their estimates.

Our Take On f-code's Profit Performance

As we mentioned previously, the f-code's profit was hampered by unusual items in the last year. Based on this observation, we consider it possible that f-code's statutory profit actually understates its earnings potential! And on top of that, its earnings per share have grown at an extremely impressive rate over the last three years. The goal of this article has been to assess how well we can rely on the statutory earnings to reflect the company's potential, but there is plenty more to consider. With this in mind, we wouldn't consider investing in a stock unless we had a thorough understanding of the risks. Our analysis shows 4 warning signs for f-code (1 is concerning!) and we strongly recommend you look at them before investing.

This note has only looked at a single factor that sheds light on the nature of f-code's profit. But there are plenty of other ways to inform your opinion of a company. Some people consider a high return on equity to be a good sign of a quality business. So you may wish to see this free collection of companies boasting high return on equity, or this list of stocks with high insider ownership.

Valuation is complex, but we're here to simplify it.

data.title: Here's Why JMDC (TSE:4483) Has Caught The Eye Of Investors
data.description:
Investors are often guided by the idea of discovering 'the next big thing', even if that means buying 'story stocks' without any revenue, let alone profit. Unfortunately, these high risk investments often have little probability of ever paying off, and many investors pay a price to learn their lesson. Loss-making companies are always racing against time to reach financial sustainability, so investors in these companies may be taking on more risk than they should.

If this kind of company isn't your style, you like companies that generate revenue, and even earn profits, then you may well be interested in JMDC (TSE:4483). Now this is not to say that the company presents the best investment opportunity around, but profitability is a key component to success in business.

Trump has pledged to "unleash" American oil and gas and these 15 US stocks have developments that are poised to benefit.

Advertisement

JMDC's Earnings Per Share Are Growing

If you believe that markets are even vaguely efficient, then over the long term you'd expect a company's share price to follow its earnings per share (EPS) outcomes. Therefore, there are plenty of investors who like to buy shares in companies that are growing EPS. Shareholders will be happy to know that JMDC's EPS has grown 19% each year, compound, over three years. As a general rule, we'd say that if a company can keep up that sort of growth, shareholders will be beaming.

One way to double-check a company's growth is to look at how its revenue, and earnings before interest and tax (EBIT) margins are changing. The good news is that JMDC is growing revenues, and EBIT margins improved by 4.7 percentage points to 22%, over the last year. Ticking those two boxes is a good sign of growth, in our book.

You can take a look at the company's revenue and earnings growth trend, in the chart below. Click on the chart to see the exact numbers.

Check out our latest analysis for JMDC

In investing, as in life, the future matters more than the past. So why not check out this free interactive visualization of JMDC's forecast profits?

Are JMDC Insiders Aligned With All Shareholders?

It should give investors a sense of security owning shares in a company if insiders also own shares, creating a close alignment their interests. JMDC followers will find comfort in knowing that insiders have a significant amount of capital that aligns their best interests with the wider shareholder group. Indeed, they hold JP¥4.0b worth of its stock. This considerable investment should help drive long-term value in the business. Even though that's only about 1.7% of the company, it's enough money to indicate alignment between the leaders of the business and ordinary shareholders.

Is JMDC Worth Keeping An Eye On?

If you believe that share price follows earnings per share you should definitely be delving further into JMDC's strong EPS growth. With EPS growth rates like that, it's hardly surprising to see company higher-ups place confidence in the company through continuing to hold a significant investment. The growth and insider confidence is looked upon well and so it's worthwhile to investigate further with a view to discern the stock's true value. Of course, identifying quality businesses is only half the battle; investors need to know whether the stock is undervalued. So you might want to consider this free discounted cashflow valuation of JMDC.

While opting for stocks without growing earnings and absent insider buying can yield results, for investors valuing these key metrics, here is a carefully selected list of companies in JP with promising growth potential and insider confidence.

Please note the insider transactions discussed in this article refer to reportable transactions in the relevant jurisdiction.

data.title: Is Axogen (AXGN) Quietly Redefining Nerve Repair Moats With Avance and New Portfolio Backing?
data.description:
• TimesSquare Capital Management recently added Axogen, Inc. to its U.S. Small Cap Growth Strategy, following the company’s report of US$59.9 million in fourth-quarter 2025 revenue and 21.3% year-over-year growth from its peripheral nerve repair portfolio.
  • A key element of the story is Axogen’s Avance Nerve Graft, currently the only FDA-approved implantable biological nerve allograft, which underscores its differentiated position in peripheral nerve regeneration.
  • We’ll now examine how Axogen’s inclusion in a prominent small cap growth portfolio shapes the company’s existing investment narrative and risk profile.

Find 58 companies with promising cash flow potential yet trading below their fair value.

Advertisement

Axogen Investment Narrative Recap

To own Axogen, you need to believe that biologic nerve repair will keep gaining share in surgical practice and that Avance Nerve Graft can retain its leadership as the only FDA approved implantable biological nerve allograft. TimesSquare Capital’s inclusion of Axogen in its U.S. Small Cap Growth Strategy reinforces that narrative but does not materially change the key near term catalyst of executing the BLA transition or the central risk of heavy dependence on a single core portfolio.

The recent FDA approval of the Avance Biologics License Application, with commercial availability expected in early Q2 2026, is the announcement most closely tied to this news. It strengthens the product’s regulatory footing and supports the small cap growth thesis, but it also ushers in new operational and compliance demands that could affect margins and execution in the short term as Axogen moves fully into a biologics framework.

Yet behind the growth story, investors still need to be aware of the risk that...

data.title: Why Vericel (VCEL) Is Up 11.8% After Landing Long-Term BARDA Deal For NexoBrid
data.description:

  • A key feature of this agreement is dedicated funding for NexoBrid purchases into the U.S. Strategic National Stockpile and early work on a potential blast trauma indication, tying Vericel’s burn-care franchise directly to long-term federal preparedness priorities.

Among recent announcements, the FDA’s approval of MACI commercial manufacturing at Vericel’s Burlington facility in March 2026 stands out next to the BARDA news, because it formalizes the capacity needed to support higher MACI volumes and any future label or geographic expansion. Together with the BARDA contract, it highlights how Vericel is investing behind its existing platforms rather than broadening into new therapeutic areas.

data.title: How Investors May Respond To Duke Energy (DUK) Focusing On Regulated Infrastructure And Efficiency Initiatives
data.description:
• In late March 2026, Duke Energy received positive dam safety inspection correspondence from the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission and secured South Carolina approval to build a highly efficient, lower‑water‑use natural gas plant in Anderson County.
  • Together with the completed sale of its Tennessee gas distribution business, these developments highlight Duke Energy’s focus on regulated infrastructure, operational safety and long-term regional energy needs.

The latest dam safety inspections and the Anderson County gas plant approval support the operational and regulatory backdrop but do not materially change the near term focus on rate cases as a key catalyst, or on rising capital and financing needs as a central risk.

The most relevant update here is the South Carolina Public Service Commission’s approval for the new US$2.5 billion Anderson County natural gas plant, which ties directly into Duke’s core catalyst of growing regulated infrastructure to meet regional demand under frameworks like the Energy Security Act. While that investment supports long term reliability and potential rate base growth, it also sits squarely in the context of higher capital intensity, external financing dependence and the ongoing debate around customer affordability and rate pressure.

data.title: A Look At Migdal Insurance And Financial Holdings (TASE:MGDL) Valuation After Strong Full Year 2025 Results
data.description:
Advertisement

Full year earnings put Migdal’s recent share performance in focus

Migdal Insurance and Financial Holdings (TASE:MGDL) recently reported full year 2025 earnings, with net income of ₪1,832 million and basic earnings per share from continuing operations of ₪1.73.

Those figures compare with net income of ₪1,419 million and basic earnings per share of ₪1.35 a year earlier, giving investors fresh numbers to weigh against the stock’s recent price moves.

See our latest analysis for Migdal Insurance and Financial Holdings.

The latest earnings arrive in a mixed price backdrop, with a 1 day share price return of 2.24% and a 30 day share price decline of 14.52%. However, a very large 1 year total shareholder return suggests longer term momentum remains strong.

If Migdal’s moves have you thinking about where else capital might work hard for you, this could be a good moment to scan for financial groups within 96 top founder-led companies

With earnings per share at ₪1.73 and a 1 year total shareholder return that is very large, alongside a recent 14.52% 30 day decline, is Migdal now trading below its worth or has the market already priced in future growth?

Preferred P/E of 9.5x: Is it justified?

Migdal is trading on a P/E of 9.5x, which sits below both the Israeli market and local insurance peers, even after a very large 1 year total return.

The P/E ratio compares the current share price to earnings per share, so it gives you a quick sense of how much investors are paying for each ₪1 of profit.

For Migdal, a 9.5x P/E alongside earnings growth of 5.4% per year over 5 years and improved net profit margins from 12.7% to 14.9% suggests the market is not paying a premium for that track record. The SWS DCF model, however, estimates a future cash flow value of ₪8.69. At a last close of ₪16.42 the share price sits well above that cash flow based fair value and points to a very different signal from the earnings multiple.

Compared with the Israeli market P/E of 15.7x, Migdal sits at a clear discount. It also trades below the Asian insurance industry average of 11.2x and a peer average of 11.1x. Those gaps indicate investors are paying less for Migdal’s earnings than for both the wider market and closer peers, even though earnings growth over the past year of 29.1% exceeded its own 5 year average.

See what the numbers say about this price — find out in our valuation breakdown.

Result: Price-to-earnings of 9.5x (UNDERVALUED).

However, short term share price weakness, a 14.52% 30 day decline, and the intrinsic discount to the SWS DCF value could challenge a simple undervaluation argument.

Find out about the key risks to this Migdal Insurance and Financial Holdings narrative.

Another view: cash flows point the other way

While the P/E of 9.5x suggests Migdal trades at a discount to the Israeli market and insurance peers, the SWS DCF model tells a different story. With a future cash flow value of ₪8.69 versus a ₪16.42 share price, that framework flags the shares as overvalued. This leaves you to decide which lens matters more.

Look into how the SWS DCF model arrives at its fair value.

Simply Wall St performs a discounted cash flow (DCF) on every stock in the world every day (check out Migdal Insurance and Financial Holdings for example). We show the entire calculation in full. You can track the result in your watchlist or portfolio and be alerted when this changes, or use our stock screener to discover 244 high quality undervalued stocks. If you save a screener we even alert you when new companies match - so you never miss a potential opportunity.

Next Steps

data.title: Stronger Q3 Results And Higher Revenue Guidance Might Change The Case For Investing In Cintas (CTAS)
data.description:
• Cintas Corporation recently reported third-quarter results for the period ended February 28, 2026, with higher sales, revenue, net income and earnings per share compared to a year earlier, and also raised its fiscal 2026 revenue guidance to a range of US$11.21 billion to US$11.24 billion.
  • This combination of stronger reported performance and higher full-year expectations suggests the company is seeing healthy demand across its uniform and business services portfolio.
  • Next, we’ll examine how this upgraded full-year revenue guidance influences Cintas’ existing investment narrative and outlook assumptions.

The latest GPUs need a type of rare earth metal called Neodymium and there are only 26 companies in the world exploring or producing it. Find the list for free.

Advertisement

Cintas Investment Narrative Recap

To own Cintas, you need to believe in the long term relevance of outsourced uniforms and facility services, even as workplaces evolve and automation spreads. The latest third quarter beat and higher fiscal 2026 revenue guidance support the near term catalyst of steady demand and operational execution, but they do not remove the key risk that structural shifts toward remote work and more automated industries could gradually erode its core customer base.

The most relevant recent announcement is Cintas’ decision on March 25, 2026 to raise full year revenue guidance to US$11.21 billion to US$11.24 billion. That upgraded range, coming alongside higher sales, net income and earnings per share for both the quarter and year to date, reinforces the existing catalyst that expansion in value added services and customer outsourcing can still drive growth, even as investors weigh longer term threats from changing work patterns and technology.

Yet against this solid guidance, investors should also be aware of how rising automation could shrink the underlying demand for human dependent services over time...

Read the full narrative on Cintas (it's free!)

Cintas' narrative projects $13.6 billion revenue and $2.6 billion earnings by 2029. This requires 7.3% yearly revenue growth and about a $0.7 billion earnings increase from $1.9 billion today.

Uncover how Cintas' forecasts yield a $214.24 fair value, a 23% upside to its current price.

Exploring Other Perspectives

Before this update, the most optimistic analysts were already modeling revenue around US$13.2 billion and earnings of about US$2.5 billion by 2028, which is far more upbeat than consensus. If you compare that bullish view to the current guidance increase and to concerns about automation reducing long term uniform demand, you can see how reasonable people can disagree and why it makes sense to explore more than one storyline for Cintas.

data.title: Lucid Group (LCID) Is Up 8.9% After Reaffirming 2026 Output And Upgrading Gravity SUV Features
data.description:
• Lucid Group, Inc. reported past first-quarter 2026 operating results, producing 5,500 vehicles and delivering 3,093, while reaffirming full-year production guidance after resolving a 29-day supplier-related interruption to Gravity SUV deliveries.
  • At the same time, Lucid enhanced its 2027 Gravity lineup with added standard technology and comfort features, underscoring Gravity’s central role in its near-term growth plans and positioning in the premium SUV segment.
  • Against this backdrop, we’ll examine how reaffirmed production targets and Gravity’s expanded feature set influence Lucid’s existing investment narrative.

Outshine the giants: these 21 early-stage AI stocks could fund your retirement.

Advertisement

Lucid Group Investment Narrative Recap

To own Lucid today, you need to believe it can scale Gravity and future models fast enough to narrow deep losses while managing heavy capital needs. The Q1 2026 update, with 5,500 vehicles produced, 3,093 delivered, and full year guidance reaffirmed despite a 29 day Gravity interruption, slightly improves confidence in execution, but does not materially change the biggest near term risk around persistent negative margins and the need for fresh funding.

The most relevant fresh data point is Lucid’s confirmation of its 2026 production target of 25,000 to 27,000 units at its recent Investor Day. That target, combined with the enhanced 2027 Gravity lineup and ongoing Uber and Nuro fleet plans, keeps volume ramp as the key short term catalyst, while the gap between production and deliveries and past margin pressure remain central issues investors will watch as earnings are reported.

Yet investors should also weigh how quickly a resolved 29 day Gravity halt could still expose Lucid to renewed supply chain fragility that investors should be aware of...

Lucid Group's narrative projects $5.6 billion revenue and $285.8 million earnings by 2028.

data.title: Is IMV Corporation (TSE:7760) Potentially Undervalued?
data.description:
While IMV Corporation (TSE:7760) might not have the largest market cap around , it saw a significant share price rise of 35% in the past couple of months on the TSE. While good news for shareholders, the company has traded much higher in the past year. As a small cap stock, hardly covered by any analysts, there is generally more of an opportunity for mispricing as there is less activity to push the stock closer to fair value. Is there still an opportunity here to buy? Let’s examine IMV’s valuation and outlook in more detail to determine if there’s still a bargain opportunity.

We've found 21 US stocks that are forecast to pay a dividend yield of over 6% next year. See the full list for free.

Advertisement

Is IMV Still Cheap?

According to our valuation model, IMV seems to be fairly priced at around 13% below our intrinsic value, which means if you buy IMV today, you’d be paying a fair price for it. And if you believe the company’s true value is ¥3432.72, then there’s not much of an upside to gain from mispricing. Although, there may be an opportunity to buy in the future. This is because IMV’s beta (a measure of share price volatility) is high, meaning its price movements will be exaggerated relative to the rest of the market. If the market is bearish, the company’s shares will likely fall by more than the rest of the market, providing a prime buying opportunity.

View our latest analysis for IMV

What kind of growth will IMV generate?

Future outlook is an important aspect when you’re looking at buying a stock, especially if you are an investor looking for growth in your portfolio. Buying a great company with a robust outlook at a cheap price is always a good investment, so let’s also take a look at the company's future expectations. However, with a relatively muted profit growth of 9.4% expected over the next couple of years, growth doesn’t seem like a key driver for a buy decision for IMV, at least in the short term.

What This Means For You

Are you a shareholder? It seems like the market has already priced in 7760’s future outlook, with shares trading around its fair value. However, there are also other important factors which we haven’t considered today, such as the track record of its management team. Have these factors changed since the last time you looked at the stock? Will you have enough confidence to invest in the company should the price drop below its fair value?

Are you a potential investor? If you’ve been keeping tabs on 7760, now may not be the most advantageous time to buy, given it is trading around its fair value. However, the positive outlook means it’s worth further examining other factors such as the strength of its balance sheet, in order to take advantage of the next price drop.

In light of this, if you'd like to do more analysis on the company, it's vital to be informed of the risks involved. At Simply Wall St, we found 1 warning sign for IMV and we think they deserve your attention.

If you are no longer interested in IMV, you can use our free platform to see our list of over 50 other stocks with a high growth potential.
